name: claude-for-hk-law
description: Reference agents, skills, and data connectors for Hong Kong legal workflows — covering commercial, privacy, product, corporate, employment, litigation, regulatory, AI governance, IP, and the learning side of practice (law school clinics and students).

plugins.name: commercial-legal
plugins.description: Reviews vendor agreements, NDAs, and SaaS subscriptions under Hong Kong contract law (common law, Cap 26 Sale of Goods Ordinance). Tracks renewals and cancel-by deadlines, routes escalations to the right approver, and translates reviews into summaries business stakeholders will read — all within Hong Kong court jurisdiction.

plugins.name: privacy-legal
plugins.description: Triages processing activities, generates PIAs, reviews DPAs as controller or processor, drafts DSAR responses within PDPO (Cap 486) timelines, and monitors policy drift against practice — covering cross-border data transfer requirements and the six DPPs.

plugins.name: product-legal
plugins.description: Reviews product launches against your risk calibration, answers 'is this a problem?' questions in minutes, checks marketing copy against the Trade Descriptions Ordinance (Cap 362) and HK consumer protection law, and flags upcoming launches that need legal eyes before anyone asks.

plugins.name: corporate-legal
plugins.description: Runs M&A diligence under the Companies Ordinance (Cap 622), builds disclosure schedules and closing checklists, drafts board consents and minutes in HK format, and tracks entity compliance deadlines — including winding-up procedures under Cap 32.

plugins.name: employment-legal
plugins.description: Reviews hires and terminations for HK-specific risk flags under the Employment Ordinance (Cap 57), MPF requirements, and anti-discrimination ordinances (DDO, SDO, FDO). Classifies workers, tracks leave deadlines, runs internal investigations, and drafts policies consistent with HK labour law.

plugins.name: regulatory-legal
plugins.description: Watches regulatory feeds from the SFC, HKMA, and IA, diffs new rules against your policy library, tracks consultation deadlines and open gaps, and writes the digest your team reads Monday morning — covering Hong Kong's dual-securities and banking regulatory framework.

plugins.name: ai-governance-legal
plugins.description: Triages proposed AI use cases against your registry, runs impact assessments under the PDPO amendments, reviews vendor AI terms for training-on-data and liability gaps, and keeps your AI policy current with PCPD guidance and emerging HK AI regulation.

plugins.name: litigation-legal
plugins.description: Manages the litigation portfolio — matters, deadlines, holds, demands, outside counsel — under HK civil procedure (HCR, DCAC). Covers the CFA/CFI/DC court structure, claim charts, chronologies, depo prep, privilege logs, and brief drafting. Adapts to how you work litigation: in-house, firm, or solo.

plugins.name: law-student
plugins.description: Drills Socratically, briefs cases, builds outlines, runs PCLL and bar prep sessions tuned to HK requirements (HKU, CUHK, CityU), grades IRAC practice, and plans the study schedule — without ever writing it for you.

plugins.name: legal-clinic
plugins.description: Sets up the clinic, onboards students, runs structured intake, tracks deadlines with malpractice-aware caution, and hands off cases at semester end — built for HK community legal clinics, the duty lawyer service, and CLIC resources.

plugins.name: legal-builder-hub
plugins.description: Finds, evaluates, and installs community legal skills — with a security review gate before anything lands in your environment. Includes HK-specific template language and localised practice guidelines.

plugins.name: ip-legal
plugins.description: Runs first-pass trademark clearance under Cap 559 (Trade Marks Ordinance), patentability triage under Cap 514 (Patents Ordinance), copyright analysis under Cap 528 (Copyright Ordinance). Screens invention disclosures, drafts cease-and-desist letters, checks open source compliance, reviews IP clauses, and tracks registrations and renewal deadlines in Hong Kong.

plugins.name: cocounsel-legal
plugins.description: CoCounsel Legal delivers comprehensive Westlaw Deep Research reports with inline, linked citations to Westlaw and Practical Law sources — including Hong Kong case law and legislative materials.

plugins.name: hk-arbitration
plugins.description: Hong Kong arbitration under the Arbitration Ordinance (Cap 609), UNCITRAL Model Law, and HKIAC rules. Covers arbitration agreements, interim measures, award enforcement (domestic and China under Arrangement), and court supervision.

plugins.name: hk-basic-law
plugins.description: Hong Kong Basic Law, NPCSC interpretations, Annex III national laws, constitutional review, and the 'one country, two systems' framework. Covers the relationship between the HKSAR and the Central Government, fundamental rights and duties, and the legal framework under Articles 8, 18, 84, 158, and 159.

plugins.name: hk-commercial-law
plugins.description: Hong Kong commercial law covering the Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap 26), Control of Exemption Clauses Ordinance (Cap 71), Bills of Exchange Ordinance (Cap 19), and common law contract and tort principles. Covers sale of goods, agency, bailment, guarantees, and commercial dispute resolution.

plugins.name: hk-companies
plugins.description: Hong Kong company law under the Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) and winding up (Cap 32). Incorporation, share capital, directors' duties, shareholder remedies, insolvency, corporate governance, and regulatory filings with the Companies Registry.

plugins.name: hk-competition
plugins.description: Hong Kong competition law under the Competition Ordinance (Cap 619). Covers the first conduct rule (anti-competitive agreements), second conduct rule (abuse of substantial market power), merger control regime (non-mandatory), Competition Commission investigations, and Competition Tribunal proceedings.

plugins.name: hk-data-privacy
plugins.description: Hong Kong data privacy under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (Cap 486). Covers data protection principles (DPP1-6), direct marketing, data breach, cross-border transfers, PCPD investigations, and compliance programmes.

plugins.name: hk-employment
plugins.description: Hong Kong employment law under the Employment Ordinance (Cap 57), Employees' Compensation Ordinance (Cap 282), MPF Schemes Ordinance (Cap 485), and anti-discrimination ordinances (Cap 480, 487, 527, 602). Covers contract drafting, termination, discrimination, workplace injury, trade unions, and Labour Tribunal procedure.

plugins.name: hk-family-law
plugins.description: Hong Kong family law under the Matrimonial Causes Ordinance (Cap 179), Matrimonial Proceedings and Property Ordinance (Cap 192), Guardianship of Minors Ordinance (Cap 13), and Separation and Maintenance Orders Ordinance (Cap 16). Covers divorce, financial relief, child custody, and adoption.

plugins.name: hk-immigration
plugins.description: Hong Kong immigration law under the Immigration Ordinance (Cap 115). Covers right of abode, visa schemes (employment, investment, talent), dependent visas, visitor conditions, deportation, and immigration appeals to the Immigration Tribunal.

plugins.name: hk-intellectual-property
plugins.description: Hong Kong intellectual property law under the Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559), Patents Ordinance (Cap 514), Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528), and Registered Designs Ordinance (Cap 522). Covers registration, enforcement, licensing, passing off, fair dealing, IPD practice, and Customs & Excise enforcement.

plugins.name: hk-litigation-procedure
plugins.description: Hong Kong civil litigation under the Rules of the High Court (Cap 4A) and District Court (Cap 336H). Covers case management, pleadings, discovery, summons, trial procedure, costs, appeals, and enforcement. Includes CFA, CA, CFI, DC procedure.

plugins.name: hk-nsl-rules
plugins.description: Hong Kong National Security Law (NSL) — Annex III national law applicable in Hong Kong. Covers NSL offences, court procedures, designated judges, police powers, bail provisions, trial procedures without jury, and implications for legal practitioners and their clients.

plugins.name: hk-property
plugins.description: Hong Kong property law under the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance (Cap 219), Land Registration Ordinance (Cap 128), Building Management Ordinance (Cap 344), and New Territories land. Covers sale and purchase, leasing, mortgages, deeds, Land Registry searches, building management, and stamp duty.

plugins.name: hk-shipping-maritime
plugins.description: Hong Kong maritime law under admiralty jurisdiction, the Bills of Lading Ordinance (Cap 440), Merchant Shipping Ordinances, and international conventions. Covers ship registration, admiralty claims, bills of lading, charterparties, marine insurance, collision, salvage, and limitation of liability.

plugins.name: hk-trusts-estate
plugins.description: Hong Kong trust and estate law under the Trusts Ordinance (Cap 29), Probate and Administration Ordinance (Cap 10), Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Ordinance (Cap 481). Covers trust creation, trustee duties, will drafting, probate, administration of estates, and will disputes.
